content:
- Finished the axum API rewrite task end to end and pushed it.
- Final task commit is 6308bc9 on master; post-commit docs publish also succeeded and pushed the mdBook sync commit to pgtuskmaster-docs.
- Validation at final tree: make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, make docs-lint, make docs-build, and make docs-hygiene all passed; Ralph task metadata was switched and the task file now has passes=true.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md after startup progress read.
- Resolved blockers at a high level: no active bug tasks listed; config simplification is still blocked by incomplete ctl story because task 09 remains undone; cert reload is blocked by ctl 08 but that blocker is done, so cert reload is actually unblocked; general architecture 01/02 are unblocked because ctl 10 is done; logging simplification and ctl 09 are unblocked.
- Need to inspect the actual candidate task files and pick the highest-priority logical next task, then write only its path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Compared the unblocked candidates. No active bug tasks exist, so selection stays in the non-bug failing branch.
- Medium priority tasks outrank low priority tasks. Between story-general-architecture 01 and 02, picked 01 because it is the story-level architecture review that is supposed to generate multiple focused follow-up tasks before the story moves deeper into one concrete implementation; the story currently only contains 01 and a single follow-up 02, so 01 still has unmet acceptance intent.
- Next action is to write `.ralph/tasks/story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/01-task-find-general-architecture-privacy-and-deduplication-improvements-and-create-follow-up-tasks.md` into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- Started a new context window.
- Read previous progress log and confirmed the last completed task was the axum API rewrite.
- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and found the active task path: .ralph/tasks/story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/01-task-find-general-architecture-privacy-and-deduplication-improvements-and-create-follow-up-tasks.md.
- Reading the task file now to determine whether this is still a TO BE VERIFIED design pass or a NOW EXECUTE implementation pass.

- Read the active task file. It is a research-and-task-creation task, not an implementation refactor.
- The generic TO BE VERIFIED type-redesign flow from the user does not map cleanly to this task because the task explicitly forbids implementation refactors and only requires creating follow-up tasks.
- Next step is to verify the DCS prerequisite task is complete, inspect the add-task-as-agent skill, and scan the repository broadly for strong architecture candidates.

- Started config subsystem survey for a follow-up refactor task.
- Read prior progress log to rehydrate context; this remains a research-only task with no file modifications.
- Next I am listing src/config and inspecting closely related callers to find a real privacy/duplication/ownership smell with concrete evidence.

- Confirmed the DCS prerequisite task is completed and marked passes=true.
- Inspected the story directory and found an existing follow-up task for shrinking runtime/node.rs.
- Spawned parallel explorer agents to survey config, logging, and the HA/process boundary for additional strong architecture-task candidates.
- Reading the same subsystems locally now to verify concrete evidence before creating new task files.

- Existing backlog already contains dedicated config-simplification and logging-simplification tasks, so I am avoiding duplicate follow-up tasks in those areas.
- Strong untracked candidates now look like the HA/process boundary and the public test_harness / duplicated given-fixture architecture.
- Reading lib.rs, integration-test imports, and the HA given directories to decide whether fixture duplication and public test support belong in one task or two.

- Read src/config/{mod,schema,parser,materialize,defaults,endpoint}. The strongest smell is not generic repetition; it is duplicated ownership of postgres usernames across connection identities and role configs.
- Evidence: PostgresConnIdentityConfig stores user/dbname/ssl settings, parser requires identity.user then validate_runtime_config enforces local_conn_identity.user == roles.superuser.username and rewind_conn_identity.user == roles.rewinder.username.
- Evidence from callers: runtime/node builds local conninfo from local_conn_identity plus a separate superuser username argument, and uses the separate username instead of identity.user. postgres_roles also uses roles.superuser.username with local_conn_identity.dbname. This makes identity.user redundant in runtime behavior.
- Secondary smell exists around InlineOrPath/SecretSource representation leakage to callers, but the role/identity ownership split looks like the best focused follow-up task.
- Next I will produce the final recommendation with file/function references and scope boundaries, without modifying files.

- Surveyed src/ha/*, src/process/*, src/runtime/node.rs, and related api wiring for a focused architecture smell at the HA/process boundary.
- Strongest issue found: HA owns too much process-specific and secret-bearing configuration (`ProcessDispatchDefaults`) and also owns a duplicated process command/status vocabulary (`ReconcileAction`, HA-local `ProcessState`, `JobKind`, HA `ShutdownMode`).
- Concrete evidence: src/ha/state.rs holds replicator/rewinder auth in HA context; src/runtime/node.rs injects them from RuntimeConfig; src/ha/source_conn.rs builds process source connections with secrets from HA-owned defaults; src/ha/process_dispatch.rs materializes concrete ProcessJobKind specs inside HA; src/ha/types.rs mirrors process job/status enums and includes lossy mappings from process ActiveJobKind.
- Supporting ADT smell: `ReconcileAction` mixes process commands with DCS/publication/role side effects, then `ha::worker::execute_action` branches them and `ha::process_dispatch::dispatch_process_action` has an UnsupportedAction arm for non-process variants. This makes impossible states representable and shows the boundary is not actually explicit.
- Independent follow-up task should keep HA decide/reconcile behavior the same while introducing a narrower HA->process command/intention boundary owned outside HA, and removing secret-bearing auth/defaults from HaWorkerCtx.

- Surveyed src/logging/* plus immediate callers/tests/docs without editing files.
- Strongest concrete issue is a logging privacy/duplication smell in raw payload ingestion: postgres jsonlog stores the normalized message and also the full original JSON object; raw parse failures store the message and the same raw line again; subprocess non-UTF8 paths store a rendered message plus raw_bytes_hex. Sinks serialize the whole LogRecord verbatim.
- Also noted a smaller ownership smell: postgres ingest owns full RuntimeConfig and runtime::node clones the full config just to launch the ingest worker, but that is secondary to the payload-boundary issue.
- Next step is to answer with exact file/function references, explain why the privacy/duplication refactor is independently actionable, and propose a narrow task title/scope without creating files because the user explicitly said not to modify anything.

- Finished evidence gathering for three follow-up tasks.
- Decided to create tasks for: shrinking the HA->process boundary, removing public test_harness leakage from the production library surface, and collapsing duplicated HA given fixtures into a typed topology/fixture pipeline.
- Next I am editing the story task directory to add those task markdown files in the add-task-as-agent format.

- make check, make lint, and make test passed.
- make test-long failed in the ultra-long HA suite even though this task only changed markdown task files.
- Investigating nextest junit/log artifacts now to identify the specific failing test and determine whether this is a baseline repo failure.

- Created three follow-up architecture tasks under story-general-architecture-improvement-finding for the HA/process boundary, public test_harness leakage, and duplicated HA given fixtures.
- Validation status: make check passed, make lint passed, make test passed.
- make test-long failed twice in the same HA scenario: ha_primary_storage_stalled_then_new_primary_takes_over, with authority=no_primary(leaseopen) while waiting for a replacement primary after wedging the initial primary. A single targeted rerun passed in between, so the failure appears timing-sensitive but real.
- Created bug file .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-ha-primary-storage-stalled-then-new-primary-takes-over-can-stall-with-no-authoritative-primary.md to record the blocker.
- Did not mark the active research task as passes=true, did not switch task, and did not commit/push because the required all-green gate is not satisfied yet.

- Rehydrated context by reading the codex progress log and current task pointer.
- Current task is .ralph/tasks/story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/01-task-find-general-architecture-privacy-and-deduplication-improvements-and-create-follow-up-tasks.md.
- Prior state says the task work itself was done, but final completion was blocked because make test-long exposed a likely flaky/baseline HA failure; reading the task file now to confirm required next action.

- Verified the DCS prerequisite task is complete with passes=true.
- Confirmed the follow-up task files created for this story exist in the expected directory (runtime/node narrowing already existed; this task added three more files plus a bug file for the flaky HA scenario).
- Sampling the created task markdown now to ensure they are self-contained and match the research-only scope before rerunning the full make validation gate.

- Confirmed the created follow-up tasks are present and self-contained.
- Starting the required validation sequence now: make check, make lint, make test, make test-long.
- Known risk from previous turn is a timing-sensitive failure in the ultra-long HA suite; I will only close the task if all required targets pass cleanly in this run.

- make check passed cleanly.
- Proceeding with make lint next, then make test and make test-long.
- No code edits needed so far; this turn is validation/closeout unless the flaky ultra-long HA failure reproduces again.

- make lint passed cleanly after the full docs/lint/clippy gate.
- Starting make test now.
- If that passes, only make test-long remains before this task can be marked complete.

- make test passed cleanly: 206 tests run, 206 passed, 26 skipped.
- Starting make test-long now. This is the last validation gate and previously showed a timing-sensitive HA failure in ha_primary_storage_stalled_then_new_primary_takes_over.
- If make test-long passes, I can update the task metadata, switch Ralph, commit, and push.

- make test-long is in progress; HA image setup passed and the long-running HA scenarios are executing.
- No new output yet after 30 seconds, which is normal for this suite.
- Remaining uncertainty is whether the previously flaky ha_primary_storage_stalled_then_new_primary_takes_over scenario reproduces.

- make test-long passed cleanly: 26 ultra-long HA tests run, 26 passed, 4 skipped.
- The previously flaky ha_primary_storage_stalled_then_new_primary_takes_over scenario passed in the full suite.
- Entering final closeout: docs check via k2-docs-loop skill, then task metadata update, Ralph task switch, commit, and push.

- Updated the active task file to status=completed, passes=true, and checked all acceptance criteria.
- Decided no docs/src update is required because this task only added Ralph backlog/task metadata and did not change runtime behavior or user-facing features.
- Running .ralph/task_switch.sh now, then I will commit all task/progress/current-task artifacts and push.
